title: Platinum Edition
wikitext:
[]: Platinum Edition             Quote Post by Dave » Mon Jun 21, 2004 8:15 pm Not sure where this post should go, but I guess this could be considered news. Anyway, anyone got any information on the new Evangelion Platinum Edition?

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Mon Jun 21, 2004 9:31 pm http://www.evamonkey.com/2004_04_01_archive.php

[]:             Quote Post by Maverick » Mon Jun 21, 2004 9:36 pm And here's a pic of the box. http://www.animatedbliss.com/images/DVD/Neon%20Genesis%20Evangelion/lg_neon-genesis-evangelion_platinum-01-box.jpg IMO, it's quite nice.

[]: Not to sound bitter...             Quote Post by Dennis » Mon Jun 21, 2004 10:00 pm I was reading in Newtype about the Evangelion platinum edition and I thought that it sounded like a plot my ADV to bilk loyal EVA fans of their hard-earned money. Besides having the Director's Cut episodes, what else is special about Platinum?

[]:             Quote Post by Maverick » Mon Jun 21, 2004 10:09 pm Well... they come with remastered video and audio (5.1). They may even come with some translated extras.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jun 21, 2004 10:29 pm Here's another press release from ADV. This one pertains directly to volume 1. http://www.evamonkey.com/writings_adv6.php

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Tue Jun 22, 2004 1:44 am Whoa that box looks awesome. I wonder if there is a way to buy just the box without the dvds in it.

[]:             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Tue Jun 22, 2004 5:52 am Whoa, I didn't know there was going to be a box! I want it, it looks hot. I wonder, will it be one of those "First DVD comes with the box" deals? Edit: Just checked Anime Nation. Right now you can pre-order the first DVD with the box for only $30.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Tue Jun 22, 2004 6:23 am That would be nice... I think I may just keep my Perfect Collection box, since I really like it, and just put the Platinum DVDs in that. But the box is nice... hmmm...

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Tue Jun 22, 2004 3:16 pm I think i'll keep my money, I don't want to pay 200 bucks for a series I already have.

[]:             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Tue Jun 22, 2004 3:49 pm I do, because I'm insane.

[]:             Quote Post by Slade » Wed Jun 23, 2004 3:48 am Well, it all works out for me because I never got the original eva DVDs. So now is my chance 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Vulkurt » Wed Jun 23, 2004 10:11 am coff wrote: I think i'll keep my money, I don't want to pay 200 bucks for a series I already have.

[]:             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Wed Jun 23, 2004 11:46 am I'm going to keep mine. So when I make my shrine, it won't be missing anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Asterix » Wed Jun 23, 2004 2:34 pm Does anybody know if it's going to be a cardboardish box like the Perfect Collection one, or a laser-engraved limited edition set like the first run of Paul Champagne ones? Cause I may just get it if it's a metal one, in part just to say I have both metal boxes...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jun 23, 2004 3:34 pm If ADV would be kind enough to send me a review copy prior to the release, I would tell you. Sadly I have not even gotten a response to my emails. And there have been several emails, over the last month.

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Wed Jun 23, 2004 11:32 pm Vulkurt wrote: even though I do like to have the best edition of anything that I own.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Jun 23, 2004 11:33 pm When they release the super platinum edition ten years from now, I'll buy it along with millions of other fans.

[]:             Quote Post by Super Mega Ultra Deluxe » Wed Jun 23, 2004 11:46 pm coff wrote: How is Platinum any better than the Perfect collection? It's the same damn series. I could care less about the "remastered" video or whatever, and all the other extras. And what happens 10 years from now when they release the "Super Platinum edition"?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jun 23, 2004 11:48 pm I'll just say that I've seen the NewType Renewal compararison video, and the video and audio quality is very very nice. It was almost like watching it again for the first time. It just felt more immersive for some reason, more compelling. The Renewal set includes new stuff, but no new animation. There are little things here and there you notice, for instance, in the first episode, when Shinji refuses to pilot Unit 01, the Eva's eyes turn off, and then after it activates to protect him from the falling debris, he looks up at it and its eyes turn back on. It was a very intriguing alteration, I can't wait to see what else was altered.
summary:

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Mon Jun 21, 2004 9:31 pm http://www.evamonkey.com/2004_04_01_archive.php
